_notes: Major fertilizer production facilities in and dependent on the Persian Gulf. The cascade: LNG shortage → ammonia plants shut → urea/ammonium nitrate production falls → global fertilizer price spike → farmers reduce application → crop yields fall.

gulf_producers.name: Qatar Fertiliser Company (QAFCO)
gulf_producers.notes: World's largest single urea production complex. Offline due to Ras Laffan disruption. Yara is one of world's largest fertilizer companies — this is a critical input to their global supply chain.

gulf_producers.name: SABIC Jubail Complex
gulf_producers.notes: Jubail Industrial City is Saudi Arabia's main petrochemical hub. Products must still transit Hormuz for export despite being on Gulf coast.

gulf_producers.name: Fertiglobe / OCI Ruwais
gulf_producers.notes: Located at Ruwais on UAE coast. Can use Fujairah routing. Fertiglobe is a key global ammonia/urea exporter — one of the few Gulf producers with partial bypass.

gulf_producers.name: Oman India Fertiliser Company (OMIFCO)
gulf_producers.notes: Oman is outside the strait. Direct Indian ownership means product primarily goes to India. One of the few major Gulf fertilizer producers unaffected by Hormuz closure.

dependent_consumers.name: India Fertilizer Industry
dependent_consumers.notes: India is world's second-largest urea consumer. Heavily dependent on Gulf natural gas imports for domestic ammonia production. Facilities using imported gas face shutdown when LNG price exceeds economics.

dependent_consumers.name: Bangladesh Fertilizer Industry
dependent_consumers.notes: Over 50% of fertilizer from Gulf. Severe vulnerability — Bangladesh agricultural productivity at risk with sustained closure.

dependent_consumers.name: Pakistan Fertilizer Industry
dependent_consumers.notes: ~50% of fertilizer imports from Gulf. Pakistan already food-stressed — fertilizer shortage creates compounded risk.

dependent_consumers.name: The Mosaic Company (US/Brazil)
dependent_consumers.notes: World's largest phosphate producer. Relies on Gulf sulfur for sulfuric acid production used in phosphate fertilizer. Gulf sulfur supply disruption forces production cuts in US and Brazil.

commodity_cascade.description: The fertilizer cascade that emerges from LNG/natural gas disruption
steps: LNG supply disrupted → LNG/gas price spike
Gas price > economic threshold → ammonia synthesis plants shut down (Haber-Bosch process requires ~800-900°C with natural gas as both feedstock and fuel)
Ammonia shortage → urea and ammonium nitrate production falls (urea = CO2 + 2NH3)
Sulfur shortage (Gulf = 50% of seaborne sulfur) → sulfuric acid shortage → phosphate fertilizer production falls (Americas, North Africa affected)
Urea + phosphate price spike → farmers in price-sensitive markets reduce application rates
Reduced application (FAO elasticity ~0.3-0.6) → crop yield decline 4-8% for each 30% reduction in application
Yield decline + reduced food trade flows → food price spike → food insecurity

timing.price_to_farmer_decision: weeks_to_months (seasonal — spring planting window most critical)
